signals.headline: US Naval Blockade of Iranian Ports Begins | YES | HIGH confidence**
signals.raw:
US Naval Blockade of Iranian Ports Begins | YES | HIGH confidence**

The United States has initiated a blockade of Iranian ports in the Strait of Hormuz[4]. This move prompted Russia's Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov to contact his Iranian counterpart Abbas Araghchi, urging the US to "demonstrate seriousness in advancing the diplomatic process"[1].

signals.headline: US-Iran Ceasefire Talks Collapse | YES | HIGH confidence**
signals.raw:
US-Iran Ceasefire Talks Collapse | YES | HIGH confidence**

American negotiators left Islamabad on April 12 without reaching an agreement after 21 hours of discussions[2]. Vice President JD Vance stated that Iran refused to accept American terms aimed at preventing nuclear weapons development[2]. This represents a significant breakdown in diplomatic efforts during what was described as day five of

signals.headline: Russia Signals Support for Iran | YES | HIGH confidence**
signals.raw:
Russia Signals Support for Iran | YES | HIGH confidence**

Beyond diplomatic communication, Russia has allegedly provided satellite intelligence to Iran to help target Israeli energy sites, according to Ukrainian claims[6]. Lavrov also indicated Russia is prepared to assist in resolving the crisis diplomatically[3].

VANCE'S IRAN DIPLOMACY FAILS** | NO (deal not reached) | HIGH confidence

Vice President JD Vance returned empty-handed from Pakistan after 21 hours of negotiations failed to produce an agreement with Tehran to end the Iran war.[2] This represents a significant diplomatic setback for the Trump administration's national security strategy.
